item.title: Harry Kane scores twice as England defeated Croatia 4-2
item.description: England has made a winning start to their FIFA World Cup campaign after defeating Croatia 4-2 in an entertaining Group L encounter, with captain Harry Kane scoring twice. Kane put England ahead from the penalty spot in the 12th minute, but Croatia drew level through Martin Baturina in the 36th minute.
item.content:encoded:
England has made a winning start to their FIFA World Cup campaign after defeating Croatia 4-2 in an entertaining Group L encounter, with captain Harry Kane scoring twice.

Kane put England ahead from the penalty spot in the 12th minute, but Croatia drew level through Martin Baturina in the 36th minute.

The England skipper struck again in the 42nd minute to restore his side's lead before Petar Musa equalised for Croatia in first-half injury time to send the teams into the break locked at 2-2.

England regained the advantage shortly after halftime when Jude Bellingham found the back of the net in the 47th minute, while Marcus Rashford added a fourth goal in the 85th minute to secure all three points.

The result gives England an early boost in Group L as they chase a place in the knockout stages.

England are featuring in their 17th FIFA World Cup and are aiming to win the tournament for the second time after lifting the trophy in 1966.

Croatia, who are making their seventh World Cup appearance, entered the tournament after impressive recent campaigns that saw them finish runners-up in 2018 and third in 2022.

England's next Group L match will be against Ghana next Wednesday at 8am before they face Panama next Sunday at 9am.

Croatia will take on Panama next Wednesday at 11am before meeting Ghana next Sunday at 9am in their remaining group fixtures.

Meanwhile, Portugal and DR Congo settled for a 1-all draw earlier this morning.

Ghana will face Panama at 11am while Uzbekistan will take on Colombia at 2pm.

item.title: Portugal and DR Congo play out 1-1 draw in Group K clash
item.description: Portugal and DR Congo played out a 1-1 draw in their opening FIFA World Cup Group K match this morning. Portugal got off to a flying start when Joao Neves scored in the 6th minute to give the European side an early lead.
item.content:encoded:
Portugal and DR Congo played out a 1-1 draw in their opening FIFA World Cup Group K match this morning.

Portugal got off to a flying start when Joao Neves scored in the 6th minute to give the European side an early lead.

However, DR Congo struck back just before halftime through Yoane Wissa, who found the back of the net in the fifth minute of first-half injury time to send the teams into the break locked at 1-all.

Portugal, captained by Cristiano Ronaldo, are playing in their 10th FIFA World Cup and are chasing their first World Cup title.

Ronaldo is making history as the first men's player to appear in six World Cups.

Meanwhile, DR Congo are making their first World Cup appearance in 52 years and only their second overall appearance at football's biggest tournament after competing as Zaire in 1974.

Portugal will next face Uzbekistan next Wednesday at 5pm before facing Colombia next Sunday at 11.30am.

Dr Congo will face Colombia next Wednesday at 2pm, before faciing Uzbekistan next Sunday at 11.30am.

Meanwhile in other matches today, England faces Croatia in the next hour, Ghana faces Panama at 11am while Uzbekistan takes on Colombia at 2pm.

item.title: England and Croatia deadlocked 2-2 at halftime
item.description: England and Croatia are locked at 2-2 at halftime in their FIFA World Cup Group L clash. England opened the scoring in the 12th minute when captain Harry Kane converted a penalty to give the Three Lions an early lead.
item.content:encoded:
England and Croatia are locked at 2-2 at halftime in their FIFA World Cup Group L clash.

England opened the scoring in the 12th minute when captain Harry Kane converted a penalty to give the Three Lions an early lead.

Croatia hit back in the 36th minute through Martin Baturina to level the scores at 1-1.

Kane restored England's lead in the 42nd minute with his second goal of the match before Croatia responded again in first-half injury time when Petar Musa found the back of the net in the fifth minute of added time to make it 2-2 at the break.

The result leaves the match finely balanced as both sides search for a winning start to their World Cup campaign.

England are making their 17th FIFA World Cup appearance and are chasing a second world title after winning the tournament in 1966.

Croatia are featuring in their seventh FIFA World Cup and have enjoyed strong recent success on the global stage, finishing runners-up in 2018 and securing third place in 2022.

Meanwhile, Portugal and DR Congo settled for a 1-all draw earlier this morning.

Ghana will face Panama at 11am while Uzbekistan will take on Colombia at 2pm.

item.description: The 2026 State of Origin series is headed for a decider at Suncorp Stadium after Queensland stormed home in the second half to stu

item.title: Chiefs shuffle line-up for Hurricanes showdown
Labasa ready for Fiji FACT challenge
item.description: Fresh from their stint with Bula FC in the inaugural OFC Professional League, Christopher Wasasala and Joji Vuakaca will be among

item.title: Winger Atelaite Buna returns for Drua Women’s vs Brumbies clash this Saturday
item.description: Winger Atelaite Buna will make her return for the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women when they take on the Brumbies in Round 3 of the Super Rugby Women competition at 4R Stadium , Govind Park in Ba on Saturday. Head Coach Mike Legge has named the former Jasper Williams High School student on the right wing for the highly anticipated encounter, marking her return after representing the Fiji Airways Fijiana
item.content:encoded:
Winger Atelaite Buna will make her return for the McDonald's Fiji Drua Women when they take on the Brumbies in Round 3 of the Super Rugby Women competition at 4R Stadium , Govind Park in Ba on Saturday.

Head Coach Mike Legge has named the former Jasper Williams High School student on the right wing for the highly anticipated encounter, marking her return after representing the Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s side.

Originally from Kese in Yasawa, with maternal links to Narewa in Nadi, Buna returns with six tries already to her name for the Drua and will be eager to make an immediate impact.

The match-day squad also features the potential Drua debut of Fiji Airways Fijiana 7s captain Verenaisi Ditavutu, who joined the team this week.

Legge has also welcomed back several key players, with back-rower Sulita Waisega and centre Merewairita Neivosa returning from injury, while lock Mereoni Nakesa is back in the starting line-up.

Karalaini Naisewa, Keleni Marawa and Tiana Robanakadavu form the front row, while Nakesa partners Carletta Yee in the second row.

Nunia Daunimoala and Salaseini Railumu join Waisega in the loose forwards.

Kolora Lomani will captain the side from halfback and partners Varanisese Qoro in the halves.

Repeka Tove starts on the left wing, with Buna on the right, while Josivini Naihamu and Neivosa form the midfield pairing. Litiana Vueti completes the starting XV at fullback.

The Drua Women will be looking to build momentum when they host the Brumbies in front of a home crowd in Ba.

Fans attending the match will also be treated to two curtain-raiser fixtures, with Ba Women's Rugby facing Lautoka Women at 9.30am, followed by the Drua Junior Development team taking on the Ba Rugby Under-17 side at 11.30am.

The main match between the Drua Women and the Brumbies kicks off at 2pm on Saturday.

item.title: Portugal and DR Congo are locked in at 1-1 as Ronaldo begins historic sixth World Cup
item.description: DR Congo have fought back to level their FIFA World Cup Group K match against Portugal 1-1 just before halftime. Portugal took an early lead through Joao Neves, who found the back of the net in the 6th minute to give the European side a dream start.
item.content:encoded:
DR Congo have fought back to level their FIFA World Cup Group K match against Portugal 1-1 just before halftime.

Portugal took an early lead through Joao Neves, who found the back of the net in the 6th minute to give the European side a dream start.

However, DR Congo responded in first-half injury time when Yoane Wissa scored in the fifth minute of added time to bring the African nation back into the contest.

Portugal, led by captain Cristiano Ronaldo, are playing in their 10th FIFA World Cup and are chasing their first-ever World Cup title.

41-year-old Ronaldo is making history by appearing in a record sixth World Cup and has scored 937 career goals for club and country.

DR Congo, meanwhile, are making only their second World Cup appearance and their first since 1974 when they competed as Zaire.

Meanwhile in other matches, England faces Croatia at 8am, Ghana faces Panama at 11am while Uzbekistan takes on Colombia at 2pm.

item.title: Austria end 36-year wait for World Cup win
item.description: Austria have won a match at the FIFA World Cup finals for the first time since 1990 as they beat debutants Jordan 3-1 in San Franc

item.title: Messi scores first World Cup hat-trick as Argentina beats Algeria 3-0
item.description: Lionel Messi has scored his first-ever FIFA World Cup hat-trick as Argentina defeated Algeria 3-0 this afternoon.The captain once

item.title: Messi gives Argentina 1-0 halftime lead over Algeria
item.description: Lionel Messi's first-half strike has given Argentina a 1-0 lead over Algeria at halftime in their opening FIFA World Cup Group J c
